step_by_step_thinking:
Weber-Fechner's Law suggests that the increase in intensity required for an increment in sensation (just noticeable difference or JND) increases logarithmically with increasing levels of stimulation. This means it relates more closely to how we perceive changes rather than just raw physical intensities. The perception scales non-linearly due to neural processing mechanisms which normalize increments across different baseline stimuli strengths.
